members.name: Close
members.summary: Closes the current connection, allowing reuse of this [HTTPClient].

members.name: ConnectToHost
members.signature: public int ConnectToHost(String host, int port, TLSOptions tlsOptions)
members.summary: Connects to a host. This needs to be done before any requests are sent. If no [param port] is specified (or [code]-1[/code] is used), it is automatically set to 80 for HTTP and 443 for HTTPS. You can pass the optional [param tls_options] parameter to customize the trusted certification authorities, or the common name verification when using HTTPS. See [method TLSOptions.client] and [method TLSOptions.client_unsafe].

members.name: GetResponseBodyLength
members.summary: Returns the response's body length. [b]Note:[/b] Some Web servers may not send a body length. In this case, the value returned will be [code]-1[/code]. If using chunked transfer encoding, the body length will also be [code]-1[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] This function always returns [code]-1[/code] on the Web platform due to browsers limitations.

members.name: GetResponseCode
members.summary: Returns the response's HTTP status code.

members.name: GetResponseHeadersAsDictionary
members.summary: Returns all response headers as a [Dictionary]. Each entry is composed by the header name, and a [String] containing the values separated by [code]"; "[/code]. The casing is kept the same as the headers were received. [codeblock] { "content-length": 12, "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=UTF-8", } [/codeblock]

members.name: GetStatus
members.summary: Returns a [enum Status] constant. Need to call [method poll] in order to get status updates.

members.name: HasResponse
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], this [HTTPClient] has a response available.

members.name: IsResponseChunked
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], this [HTTPClient] has a response that is chunked.

members.name: Poll
members.summary: This needs to be called in order to have any request processed. Check results with [method get_status].

members.name: QueryStringFromDict
members.summary: Generates a GET/POST application/x-www-form-urlencoded style query string from a provided dictionary, e.g.: [codeblocks] [gdscript] var fields = { "username": "user", "password": "pass" } var query_string = http_client.query_string_from_dict(fields) # Returns "username=user&password=pass" [/gdscript] [csharp] var fields = new Godot.Collections.Dictionary { { "username", "user" }, { "password", "pass" } }; string queryString = httpClient.QueryStringFromDict(fields); // Returns "username=user&password=pass" [/csharp] [/codeblocks] Furthermore, if a key has a [code]null[/code] value, only the key itself is added, without equal sign and value. If the value is an array, for each value in it a pair with the same key is added. [codeblocks] [gdscript] var fields = { "single": 123, "not_valued": null, "multiple": [22, 33, 44] } var query_string = http_client.query_string_from_dict(fields) # Returns "single=123&not_valued&multiple=22&multiple=33&multiple=44" [/gdscript] [csharp] var fields = new Godot.Collections.Dictionary { { "single", 123 }, { "notValued", default }, { "multiple", new Godot.Collections.Array { 22, 33, 44 } }, }; string queryString = httpClient.QueryStringFromDict(fields); // Returns "single=123&not_valued&multiple=22&multiple=33&multiple=44" [/csharp] [/codeblocks]

members.name: ReadResponseBodyChunk
members.summary: Reads one chunk from the response.

members.name: Request
members.signature: public int Request(int method, String url, PackedStringArray headers, String body)
members.summary: Sends an HTTP request to the connected host with the given [param method]. The URL parameter is usually just the part after the host, so for [code]https://example.com/index.php[/code], it is [code]/index.php[/code]. When sending requests to an HTTP proxy server, it should be an absolute URL. For [constant HTTPClient.METHOD_OPTIONS] requests, [code]*[/code] is also allowed. For [constant HTTPClient.METHOD_CONNECT] requests, it should be the authority component ([code]host:port[/code]). [param headers] are HTTP request headers. To create a POST request with query strings to push to the server, do: [codeblocks] [gdscript] var fields = { "username": "user", "password": "pass" } var query_string = http_client.query_string_from_dict(fields) var headers = ["Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded", "Content-Length: " + str(query_string.length())] var result = http_client.request(http_client.METHOD_POST, "/index.php", headers, query_string) [/gdscript] [csharp] var fields = new Godot.Collections.Dictionary { { "username", "user" }, { "password", "pass" } }; string queryString = new HttpClient().QueryStringFromDict(fields); string[] headers = ["Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded", $"Content-Length: {queryString.Length}"]; var result = new HttpClient().Request(HttpClient.Method.Post, "index.php", headers, queryString); [/csharp] [/codeblocks] [b]Note:[/b] The [param body] parameter is ignored if [param method] is [constant HTTPClient.METHOD_GET]. This is because GET methods can't contain request data. As a workaround, you can pass request data as a query string in the URL. See [method String.uri_encode] for an example.

members.name: RequestRaw
members.signature: public int RequestRaw(int method, String url, PackedStringArray headers, PackedByteArray body)
members.summary: Sends a raw HTTP request to the connected host with the given [param method]. The URL parameter is usually just the part after the host, so for [code]https://example.com/index.php[/code], it is [code]/index.php[/code]. When sending requests to an HTTP proxy server, it should be an absolute URL. For [constant HTTPClient.METHOD_OPTIONS] requests, [code]*[/code] is also allowed. For [constant HTTPClient.METHOD_CONNECT] requests, it should be the authority component ([code]host:port[/code]). [param headers] are HTTP request headers. Sends the body data raw, as a byte array and does not encode it in any way.

members.name: SetHttpProxy
members.signature: public void SetHttpProxy(String host, int port)
members.summary: Sets the proxy server for HTTP requests. The proxy server is unset if [param host] is empty or [param port] is -1.

members.name: SetHttpsProxy
members.signature: public void SetHttpsProxy(String host, int port)
members.summary: Sets the proxy server for HTTPS requests. The proxy server is unset if [param host] is empty or [param port] is -1.

members.name: BlockingModeEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], execution will block until all data is read from the response.

members.name: Connection
members.summary: The connection to use for this client.

members.name: ReadChunkSize
members.summary: The size of the buffer used and maximum bytes to read per iteration. See [method read_response_body_chunk].

values.name: METHOD_CONNECT
values.summary: HTTP CONNECT method. The CONNECT method establishes a tunnel to the server identified by the target resource. Rarely used.

values.name: METHOD_DELETE
values.summary: HTTP DELETE method. The DELETE method requests to delete the specified resource.

values.name: METHOD_GET
values.summary: HTTP GET method. The GET method requests a representation of the specified resource. Requests using GET should only retrieve data.

values.name: METHOD_HEAD
values.summary: HTTP HEAD method. The HEAD method asks for a response identical to that of a GET request, but without the response body. This is useful to request metadata like HTTP headers or to check if a resource exists.

values.name: METHOD_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Method] enum.

values.name: METHOD_OPTIONS
values.summary: HTTP OPTIONS method. The OPTIONS method asks for a description of the communication options for the target resource. Rarely used.

values.name: METHOD_PATCH
values.summary: HTTP PATCH method. The PATCH method is used to apply partial modifications to a resource.

values.name: METHOD_POST
values.summary: HTTP POST method. The POST method is used to submit an entity to the specified resource, often causing a change in state or side effects on the server. This is often used for forms and submitting data or uploading files.

values.name: METHOD_PUT
values.summary: HTTP PUT method. The PUT method asks to replace all current representations of the target resource with the request payload. (You can think of POST as "create or update" and PUT as "update", although many services tend to not make a clear distinction or change their meaning).

values.name: METHOD_TRACE
values.summary: HTTP TRACE method. The TRACE method performs a message loop-back test along the path to the target resource. Returns the entire HTTP request received in the response body. Rarely used.

values.name: RESPONSE_ACCEPTED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]202 Accepted[/code]. The request has been received but not yet acted upon. It is non-committal, meaning that there is no way in HTTP to later send an asynchronous response indicating the outcome of processing the request. It is intended for cases where another process or server handles the request, or for batch processing.

values.name: RESPONSE_ALREADY_REPORTED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]208 Already Reported[/code] (WebDAV). Used inside a DAV: propstat response element to avoid enumerating the internal members of multiple bindings to the same collection repeatedly.

values.name: RESPONSE_BAD_GATEWAY
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]502 Bad Gateway[/code]. The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, received an invalid response from an inbound server it accessed while attempting to fulfill the request. Usually returned by load balancers or proxies.

values.name: RESPONSE_BAD_REQUEST
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]400 Bad Request[/code]. The request was invalid. The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, invalid request contents, or deceptive request routing).

values.name: RESPONSE_CONFLICT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]409 Conflict[/code]. The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the target resource. This code is used in situations where the user might be able to resolve the conflict and resubmit the request.

values.name: RESPONSE_CONTINUE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]100 Continue[/code]. Interim response that indicates everything so far is OK and that the client should continue with the request (or ignore this status if already finished).

values.name: RESPONSE_CREATED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]201 Created[/code]. The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created as a result of it. This is typically the response sent after a PUT request.

values.name: RESPONSE_EXPECTATION_FAILED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]417 Expectation Failed[/code]. The expectation given in the request's Expect header field could not be met by at least one of the inbound servers.

values.name: RESPONSE_FAILED_DEPENDENCY
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]424 Failed Dependency[/code] (WebDAV). The method could not be performed on the resource because the requested action depended on another action and that action failed.

values.name: RESPONSE_FORBIDDEN
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]403 Forbidden[/code]. The client does not have access rights to the content, i.e. they are unauthorized, so server is rejecting to give proper response. Unlike [code]401[/code], the client's identity is known to the server.

values.name: RESPONSE_FOUND
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]302 Found[/code]. Temporary redirection. This response code means the URI of requested resource has been changed temporarily. New changes in the URI might be made in the future. Therefore, this same URI should be used by the client in future requests.

values.name: RESPONSE_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]504 Gateway Timeout[/code]. The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, did not receive a timely response from an upstream server it needed to access in order to complete the request. Usually returned by load balancers or proxies.

values.name: RESPONSE_GONE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]410 Gone[/code]. The target resource is no longer available at the origin server and this condition is likely permanent.

values.name: RESPONSE_HTTP_VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]505 HTTP Version Not Supported[/code]. The server does not support, or refuses to support, the major version of HTTP that was used in the request message.

values.name: RESPONSE_IM_A_TEAPOT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]418 I'm A Teapot[/code]. Any attempt to brew coffee with a teapot should result in the error code "418 I'm a teapot". The resulting entity body MAY be short and stout.

values.name: RESPONSE_IM_USED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]226 IM Used[/code] (WebDAV). The server has fulfilled a GET request for the resource, and the response is a representation of the result of one or more instance-manipulations applied to the current instance.

values.name: RESPONSE_INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]507 Insufficient Storage[/code]. The method could not be performed on the resource because the server is unable to store the representation needed to successfully complete the request.

values.name: RESPONSE_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]500 Internal Server Error[/code]. The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

values.name: RESPONSE_LENGTH_REQUIRED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]411 Length Required[/code]. The server refuses to accept the request without a defined Content-Length header.

values.name: RESPONSE_LOCKED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]423 Locked[/code] (WebDAV). The source or destination resource of a method is locked.

values.name: RESPONSE_LOOP_DETECTED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]508 Loop Detected[/code]. The server terminated an operation because it encountered an infinite loop while processing a request with "Depth: infinity". This status indicates that the entire operation failed.

values.name: RESPONSE_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]405 Method Not Allowed[/code]. The request's HTTP method is known by the server but has been disabled and cannot be used. For example, an API may forbid DELETE-ing a resource. The two mandatory methods, GET and HEAD, must never be disabled and should not return this error code.

values.name: RESPONSE_MISDIRECTED_REQUEST
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]421 Misdirected Request[/code]. The request was directed at a server that is not able to produce a response. This can be sent by a server that is not configured to produce responses for the combination of scheme and authority that are included in the request URI.

values.name: RESPONSE_MOVED_PERMANENTLY
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]301 Moved Permanently[/code]. Redirection. This response code means the URI of requested resource has been changed. The new URI is usually included in the response.

values.name: RESPONSE_MULTIPLE_CHOICES
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]300 Multiple Choice[/code]. The request has more than one possible responses and there is no standardized way to choose one of the responses. User-agent or user should choose one of them.

values.name: RESPONSE_MULTI_STATUS
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]207 Multi-Status[/code] (WebDAV). A Multi-Status response conveys information about multiple resources in situations where multiple status codes might be appropriate.

values.name: RESPONSE_NETWORK_AUTH_REQUIRED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]511 Network Authentication Required[/code]. The client needs to authenticate to gain network access.

values.name: RESPONSE_NON_AUTHORITATIVE_INFORMATION
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]203 Non-Authoritative Information[/code]. This response code means returned meta-information set is not exact set as available from the origin server, but collected from a local or a third party copy. Except this condition, 200 OK response should be preferred instead of this response.

values.name: RESPONSE_NOT_ACCEPTABLE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]406 Not Acceptable[/code]. The target resource does not have a current representation that would be acceptable to the user agent, according to the proactive negotiation header fields received in the request. Used when negotiation content.

values.name: RESPONSE_NOT_EXTENDED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]510 Not Extended[/code]. The policy for accessing the resource has not been met in the request. The server should send back all the information necessary for the client to issue an extended request.

values.name: RESPONSE_NOT_FOUND
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]404 Not Found[/code]. The server can not find requested resource. Either the URL is not recognized or the endpoint is valid but the resource itself does not exist. May also be sent instead of 403 to hide existence of a resource if the client is not authorized.

values.name: RESPONSE_NOT_IMPLEMENTED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]501 Not Implemented[/code]. The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request.

values.name: RESPONSE_NOT_MODIFIED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]304 Not Modified[/code]. A conditional GET or HEAD request has been received and would have resulted in a 200 OK response if it were not for the fact that the condition evaluated to [code]false[/code].

values.name: RESPONSE_NO_CONTENT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]204 No Content[/code]. There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful. The user-agent may update its cached headers for this resource with the new ones.

values.name: RESPONSE_OK
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]200 OK[/code]. The request has succeeded. Default response for successful requests. Meaning varies depending on the request: - [constant METHOD_GET]: The resource has been fetched and is transmitted in the message body. - [constant METHOD_HEAD]: The entity headers are in the message body. - [constant METHOD_POST]: The resource describing the result of the action is transmitted in the message body. - [constant METHOD_TRACE]: The message body contains the request message as received by the server.

values.name: RESPONSE_PARTIAL_CONTENT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]206 Partial Content[/code]. This response code is used because of a range header sent by the client to separate download into multiple streams.

values.name: RESPONSE_PAYMENT_REQUIRED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]402 Payment Required[/code]. This response code is reserved for future use. Initial aim for creating this code was using it for digital payment systems, however this is not currently used.

values.name: RESPONSE_PERMANENT_REDIRECT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]308 Permanent Redirect[/code]. The target resource has been assigned a new permanent URI and any future references to this resource ought to use one of the enclosed URIs.

values.name: RESPONSE_PRECONDITION_FAILED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]412 Precondition Failed[/code]. One or more conditions given in the request header fields evaluated to [code]false[/code] when tested on the server.

values.name: RESPONSE_PRECONDITION_REQUIRED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]428 Precondition Required[/code]. The origin server requires the request to be conditional.

values.name: RESPONSE_PROCESSING
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]102 Processing[/code] (WebDAV). Indicates that the server has received and is processing the request, but no response is available yet.

values.name: RESPONSE_PROXY_AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]407 Proxy Authentication Required[/code]. Similar to 401 Unauthorized, but it indicates that the client needs to authenticate itself in order to use a proxy.

values.name: RESPONSE_REQUESTED_RANGE_NOT_SATISFIABLE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]416 Requested Range Not Satisfiable[/code]. None of the ranges in the request's Range header field overlap the current extent of the selected resource or the set of ranges requested has been rejected due to invalid ranges or an excessive request of small or overlapping ranges.

values.name: RESPONSE_REQUEST_ENTITY_TOO_LARGE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]413 Entity Too Large[/code]. The server is refusing to process a request because the request payload is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

values.name: RESPONSE_REQUEST_HEADER_FIELDS_TOO_LARGE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]431 Request Header Fields Too Large[/code]. The server is unwilling to process the request because its header fields are too large. The request MAY be resubmitted after reducing the size of the request header fields.

values.name: RESPONSE_REQUEST_TIMEOUT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]408 Request Timeout[/code]. The server did not receive a complete request message within the time that it was prepared to wait.

values.name: RESPONSE_REQUEST_URI_TOO_LONG
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]414 Request-URI Too Long[/code]. The server is refusing to service the request because the request-target is longer than the server is willing to interpret.

values.name: RESPONSE_RESET_CONTENT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]205 Reset Content[/code]. The server has fulfilled the request and desires that the client resets the "document view" that caused the request to be sent to its original state as received from the origin server.

values.name: RESPONSE_SEE_OTHER
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]303 See Other[/code]. The server is redirecting the user agent to a different resource, as indicated by a URI in the Location header field, which is intended to provide an indirect response to the original request.

values.name: RESPONSE_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]503 Service Unavailable[/code]. The server is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary overload or scheduled maintenance, which will likely be alleviated after some delay. Try again later.

values.name: RESPONSE_SWITCHING_PROTOCOLS
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]101 Switching Protocol[/code]. Sent in response to an [code]Upgrade[/code] request header by the client. Indicates the protocol the server is switching to.

values.name: RESPONSE_SWITCH_PROXY
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]306 Switch Proxy[/code].

values.name: RESPONSE_TEMPORARY_REDIRECT
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]307 Temporary Redirect[/code]. The target resource resides temporarily under a different URI and the user agent MUST NOT change the request method if it performs an automatic redirection to that URI.

values.name: RESPONSE_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]429 Too Many Requests[/code]. The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time (see "rate limiting"). Back off and increase time between requests or try again later.

values.name: RESPONSE_UNAUTHORIZED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]401 Unauthorized[/code]. Credentials required. The request has not been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the target resource.

values.name: RESPONSE_UNAVAILABLE_FOR_LEGAL_REASONS
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]451 Response Unavailable For Legal Reasons[/code]. The server is denying access to the resource as a consequence of a legal demand.

values.name: RESPONSE_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]422 Unprocessable Entity[/code] (WebDAV). The server understands the content type of the request entity (hence a 415 Unsupported Media Type status code is inappropriate), and the syntax of the request entity is correct (thus a 400 Bad Request status code is inappropriate) but was unable to process the contained instructions.

values.name: RESPONSE_UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]415 Unsupported Media Type[/code]. The origin server is refusing to service the request because the payload is in a format not supported by this method on the target resource.

values.name: RESPONSE_UPGRADE_REQUIRED
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]426 Upgrade Required[/code]. The server refuses to perform the request using the current protocol but might be willing to do so after the client upgrades to a different protocol.

values.name: RESPONSE_USE_PROXY
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]305 Use Proxy[/code].

values.name: RESPONSE_VARIANT_ALSO_NEGOTIATES
values.summary: HTTP status code [code]506 Variant Also Negotiates[/code]. The server has an internal configuration error: the chosen variant resource is configured to engage in transparent content negotiation itself, and is therefore not a proper end point in the negotiation process.

values.name: STATUS_BODY
values.summary: Status: HTTP body received.

values.name: STATUS_CANT_CONNECT
values.summary: Status: Can't connect to the server.

values.name: STATUS_CANT_RESOLVE
values.summary: Status: DNS failure: Can't resolve the hostname for the given URL.

values.name: STATUS_CONNECTED
values.summary: Status: Connection established.

values.name: STATUS_CONNECTING
values.summary: Status: Currently connecting to server.

values.name: STATUS_CONNECTION_ERROR
values.summary: Status: Error in HTTP connection.

values.name: STATUS_DISCONNECTED
values.summary: Status: Disconnected from the server.

values.name: STATUS_REQUESTING
values.summary: Status: Currently sending request.

values.name: STATUS_RESOLVING
values.summary: Status: Currently resolving the hostname for the given URL into an IP.

values.name: STATUS_TLS_HANDSHAKE_ERROR
values.summary: Status: Error in TLS handshake.

constants.name: METHOD_GET
constants.summary: HTTP GET method. The GET method requests a representation of the specified resource. Requests using GET should only retrieve data.

constants.name: METHOD_HEAD
constants.summary: HTTP HEAD method. The HEAD method asks for a response identical to that of a GET request, but without the response body. This is useful to request metadata like HTTP headers or to check if a resource exists.

constants.name: METHOD_POST
constants.summary: HTTP POST method. The POST method is used to submit an entity to the specified resource, often causing a change in state or side effects on the server. This is often used for forms and submitting data or uploading files.

constants.name: METHOD_PUT
constants.summary: HTTP PUT method. The PUT method asks to replace all current representations of the target resource with the request payload. (You can think of POST as "create or update" and PUT as "update", although many services tend to not make a clear distinction or change their meaning).

constants.name: METHOD_DELETE
constants.summary: HTTP DELETE method. The DELETE method requests to delete the specified resource.

constants.name: METHOD_OPTIONS
constants.summary: HTTP OPTIONS method. The OPTIONS method asks for a description of the communication options for the target resource. Rarely used.

constants.name: METHOD_TRACE
constants.summary: HTTP TRACE method. The TRACE method performs a message loop-back test along the path to the target resource. Returns the entire HTTP request received in the response body. Rarely used.

constants.name: METHOD_CONNECT
constants.summary: HTTP CONNECT method. The CONNECT method establishes a tunnel to the server identified by the target resource. Rarely used.

constants.name: METHOD_PATCH
constants.summary: HTTP PATCH method. The PATCH method is used to apply partial modifications to a resource.

constants.name: METHOD_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Method] enum.

constants.name: STATUS_DISCONNECTED
constants.summary: Status: Disconnected from the server.

constants.name: STATUS_RESOLVING
constants.summary: Status: Currently resolving the hostname for the given URL into an IP.

constants.name: STATUS_CANT_RESOLVE
constants.summary: Status: DNS failure: Can't resolve the hostname for the given URL.

constants.name: STATUS_CONNECTING
constants.summary: Status: Currently connecting to server.

constants.name: STATUS_CANT_CONNECT
constants.summary: Status: Can't connect to the server.

constants.name: STATUS_CONNECTED
constants.summary: Status: Connection established.

constants.name: STATUS_REQUESTING
constants.summary: Status: Currently sending request.

constants.name: STATUS_BODY
constants.summary: Status: HTTP body received.

constants.name: STATUS_CONNECTION_ERROR
constants.summary: Status: Error in HTTP connection.

constants.name: STATUS_TLS_HANDSHAKE_ERROR
constants.summary: Status: Error in TLS handshake.

constants.name: RESPONSE_CONTINUE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]100 Continue[/code]. Interim response that indicates everything so far is OK and that the client should continue with the request (or ignore this status if already finished).

constants.name: RESPONSE_SWITCHING_PROTOCOLS
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]101 Switching Protocol[/code]. Sent in response to an [code]Upgrade[/code] request header by the client. Indicates the protocol the server is switching to.

constants.name: RESPONSE_PROCESSING
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]102 Processing[/code] (WebDAV). Indicates that the server has received and is processing the request, but no response is available yet.

constants.name: RESPONSE_OK
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]200 OK[/code]. The request has succeeded. Default response for successful requests. Meaning varies depending on the request: - [constant METHOD_GET]: The resource has been fetched and is transmitted in the message body. - [constant METHOD_HEAD]: The entity headers are in the message body. - [constant METHOD_POST]: The resource describing the result of the action is transmitted in the message body. - [constant METHOD_TRACE]: The message body contains the request message as received by the server.

constants.name: RESPONSE_CREATED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]201 Created[/code]. The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created as a result of it. This is typically the response sent after a PUT request.

constants.name: RESPONSE_ACCEPTED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]202 Accepted[/code]. The request has been received but not yet acted upon. It is non-committal, meaning that there is no way in HTTP to later send an asynchronous response indicating the outcome of processing the request. It is intended for cases where another process or server handles the request, or for batch processing.

constants.name: RESPONSE_NON_AUTHORITATIVE_INFORMATION
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]203 Non-Authoritative Information[/code]. This response code means returned meta-information set is not exact set as available from the origin server, but collected from a local or a third party copy. Except this condition, 200 OK response should be preferred instead of this response.

constants.name: RESPONSE_NO_CONTENT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]204 No Content[/code]. There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful. The user-agent may update its cached headers for this resource with the new ones.

constants.name: RESPONSE_RESET_CONTENT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]205 Reset Content[/code]. The server has fulfilled the request and desires that the client resets the "document view" that caused the request to be sent to its original state as received from the origin server.

constants.name: RESPONSE_PARTIAL_CONTENT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]206 Partial Content[/code]. This response code is used because of a range header sent by the client to separate download into multiple streams.

constants.name: RESPONSE_MULTI_STATUS
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]207 Multi-Status[/code] (WebDAV). A Multi-Status response conveys information about multiple resources in situations where multiple status codes might be appropriate.

constants.name: RESPONSE_ALREADY_REPORTED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]208 Already Reported[/code] (WebDAV). Used inside a DAV: propstat response element to avoid enumerating the internal members of multiple bindings to the same collection repeatedly.

constants.name: RESPONSE_IM_USED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]226 IM Used[/code] (WebDAV). The server has fulfilled a GET request for the resource, and the response is a representation of the result of one or more instance-manipulations applied to the current instance.

constants.name: RESPONSE_MULTIPLE_CHOICES
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]300 Multiple Choice[/code]. The request has more than one possible responses and there is no standardized way to choose one of the responses. User-agent or user should choose one of them.

constants.name: RESPONSE_MOVED_PERMANENTLY
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]301 Moved Permanently[/code]. Redirection. This response code means the URI of requested resource has been changed. The new URI is usually included in the response.

constants.name: RESPONSE_FOUND
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]302 Found[/code]. Temporary redirection. This response code means the URI of requested resource has been changed temporarily. New changes in the URI might be made in the future. Therefore, this same URI should be used by the client in future requests.

constants.name: RESPONSE_SEE_OTHER
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]303 See Other[/code]. The server is redirecting the user agent to a different resource, as indicated by a URI in the Location header field, which is intended to provide an indirect response to the original request.

constants.name: RESPONSE_NOT_MODIFIED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]304 Not Modified[/code]. A conditional GET or HEAD request has been received and would have resulted in a 200 OK response if it were not for the fact that the condition evaluated to [code]false[/code].

constants.name: RESPONSE_USE_PROXY
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]305 Use Proxy[/code].

constants.name: RESPONSE_SWITCH_PROXY
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]306 Switch Proxy[/code].

constants.name: RESPONSE_TEMPORARY_REDIRECT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]307 Temporary Redirect[/code]. The target resource resides temporarily under a different URI and the user agent MUST NOT change the request method if it performs an automatic redirection to that URI.

constants.name: RESPONSE_PERMANENT_REDIRECT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]308 Permanent Redirect[/code]. The target resource has been assigned a new permanent URI and any future references to this resource ought to use one of the enclosed URIs.

constants.name: RESPONSE_BAD_REQUEST
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]400 Bad Request[/code]. The request was invalid. The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, invalid request contents, or deceptive request routing).

constants.name: RESPONSE_UNAUTHORIZED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]401 Unauthorized[/code]. Credentials required. The request has not been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the target resource.

constants.name: RESPONSE_PAYMENT_REQUIRED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]402 Payment Required[/code]. This response code is reserved for future use. Initial aim for creating this code was using it for digital payment systems, however this is not currently used.

constants.name: RESPONSE_FORBIDDEN
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]403 Forbidden[/code]. The client does not have access rights to the content, i.e. they are unauthorized, so server is rejecting to give proper response. Unlike [code]401[/code], the client's identity is known to the server.

constants.name: RESPONSE_NOT_FOUND
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]404 Not Found[/code]. The server can not find requested resource. Either the URL is not recognized or the endpoint is valid but the resource itself does not exist. May also be sent instead of 403 to hide existence of a resource if the client is not authorized.

constants.name: RESPONSE_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]405 Method Not Allowed[/code]. The request's HTTP method is known by the server but has been disabled and cannot be used. For example, an API may forbid DELETE-ing a resource. The two mandatory methods, GET and HEAD, must never be disabled and should not return this error code.

constants.name: RESPONSE_NOT_ACCEPTABLE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]406 Not Acceptable[/code]. The target resource does not have a current representation that would be acceptable to the user agent, according to the proactive negotiation header fields received in the request. Used when negotiation content.

constants.name: RESPONSE_PROXY_AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]407 Proxy Authentication Required[/code]. Similar to 401 Unauthorized, but it indicates that the client needs to authenticate itself in order to use a proxy.

constants.name: RESPONSE_REQUEST_TIMEOUT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]408 Request Timeout[/code]. The server did not receive a complete request message within the time that it was prepared to wait.

constants.name: RESPONSE_CONFLICT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]409 Conflict[/code]. The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the target resource. This code is used in situations where the user might be able to resolve the conflict and resubmit the request.

constants.name: RESPONSE_GONE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]410 Gone[/code]. The target resource is no longer available at the origin server and this condition is likely permanent.

constants.name: RESPONSE_LENGTH_REQUIRED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]411 Length Required[/code]. The server refuses to accept the request without a defined Content-Length header.

constants.name: RESPONSE_PRECONDITION_FAILED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]412 Precondition Failed[/code]. One or more conditions given in the request header fields evaluated to [code]false[/code] when tested on the server.

constants.name: RESPONSE_REQUEST_ENTITY_TOO_LARGE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]413 Entity Too Large[/code]. The server is refusing to process a request because the request payload is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

constants.name: RESPONSE_REQUEST_URI_TOO_LONG
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]414 Request-URI Too Long[/code]. The server is refusing to service the request because the request-target is longer than the server is willing to interpret.

constants.name: RESPONSE_UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]415 Unsupported Media Type[/code]. The origin server is refusing to service the request because the payload is in a format not supported by this method on the target resource.

constants.name: RESPONSE_REQUESTED_RANGE_NOT_SATISFIABLE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]416 Requested Range Not Satisfiable[/code]. None of the ranges in the request's Range header field overlap the current extent of the selected resource or the set of ranges requested has been rejected due to invalid ranges or an excessive request of small or overlapping ranges.

constants.name: RESPONSE_EXPECTATION_FAILED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]417 Expectation Failed[/code]. The expectation given in the request's Expect header field could not be met by at least one of the inbound servers.

constants.name: RESPONSE_IM_A_TEAPOT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]418 I'm A Teapot[/code]. Any attempt to brew coffee with a teapot should result in the error code "418 I'm a teapot". The resulting entity body MAY be short and stout.

constants.name: RESPONSE_MISDIRECTED_REQUEST
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]421 Misdirected Request[/code]. The request was directed at a server that is not able to produce a response. This can be sent by a server that is not configured to produce responses for the combination of scheme and authority that are included in the request URI.

constants.name: RESPONSE_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]422 Unprocessable Entity[/code] (WebDAV). The server understands the content type of the request entity (hence a 415 Unsupported Media Type status code is inappropriate), and the syntax of the request entity is correct (thus a 400 Bad Request status code is inappropriate) but was unable to process the contained instructions.

constants.name: RESPONSE_LOCKED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]423 Locked[/code] (WebDAV). The source or destination resource of a method is locked.

constants.name: RESPONSE_FAILED_DEPENDENCY
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]424 Failed Dependency[/code] (WebDAV). The method could not be performed on the resource because the requested action depended on another action and that action failed.

constants.name: RESPONSE_UPGRADE_REQUIRED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]426 Upgrade Required[/code]. The server refuses to perform the request using the current protocol but might be willing to do so after the client upgrades to a different protocol.

constants.name: RESPONSE_PRECONDITION_REQUIRED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]428 Precondition Required[/code]. The origin server requires the request to be conditional.

constants.name: RESPONSE_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]429 Too Many Requests[/code]. The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time (see "rate limiting"). Back off and increase time between requests or try again later.

constants.name: RESPONSE_REQUEST_HEADER_FIELDS_TOO_LARGE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]431 Request Header Fields Too Large[/code]. The server is unwilling to process the request because its header fields are too large. The request MAY be resubmitted after reducing the size of the request header fields.

constants.name: RESPONSE_UNAVAILABLE_FOR_LEGAL_REASONS
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]451 Response Unavailable For Legal Reasons[/code]. The server is denying access to the resource as a consequence of a legal demand.

constants.name: RESPONSE_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]500 Internal Server Error[/code]. The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

constants.name: RESPONSE_NOT_IMPLEMENTED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]501 Not Implemented[/code]. The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request.

constants.name: RESPONSE_BAD_GATEWAY
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]502 Bad Gateway[/code]. The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, received an invalid response from an inbound server it accessed while attempting to fulfill the request. Usually returned by load balancers or proxies.

constants.name: RESPONSE_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]503 Service Unavailable[/code]. The server is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary overload or scheduled maintenance, which will likely be alleviated after some delay. Try again later.

constants.name: RESPONSE_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]504 Gateway Timeout[/code]. The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, did not receive a timely response from an upstream server it needed to access in order to complete the request. Usually returned by load balancers or proxies.

constants.name: RESPONSE_HTTP_VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]505 HTTP Version Not Supported[/code]. The server does not support, or refuses to support, the major version of HTTP that was used in the request message.

constants.name: RESPONSE_VARIANT_ALSO_NEGOTIATES
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]506 Variant Also Negotiates[/code]. The server has an internal configuration error: the chosen variant resource is configured to engage in transparent content negotiation itself, and is therefore not a proper end point in the negotiation process.

constants.name: RESPONSE_INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]507 Insufficient Storage[/code]. The method could not be performed on the resource because the server is unable to store the representation needed to successfully complete the request.

constants.name: RESPONSE_LOOP_DETECTED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]508 Loop Detected[/code]. The server terminated an operation because it encountered an infinite loop while processing a request with "Depth: infinity". This status indicates that the entire operation failed.

constants.name: RESPONSE_NOT_EXTENDED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]510 Not Extended[/code]. The policy for accessing the resource has not been met in the request. The server should send back all the information necessary for the client to issue an extended request.

constants.name: RESPONSE_NETWORK_AUTH_REQUIRED
constants.summary: HTTP status code [code]511 Network Authentication Required[/code]. The client needs to authenticate to gain network access.
